overview: A discussion on the liability for a scribe's error in documents, leading to a debate on the validity of selling future acquisitions and the concept of 'trust' (הימנותא) in transactions. — discussion continues into next amud.

sugyaBoundaries.gist: A discussion on the liability for a scribe's error in documents, leading to a debate on the validity of selling future acquisitions and the concept of 'trust' (הימנותא) in transactions. — discussion continues into next amud.

steps.title: Challenge: Heirs still call him a robber
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the distinction by noting that the buyer's children will still call the robber a 'robber' if the sale is invalidated.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא rejects the distinction based on the buyer's death. It argues that the heirs will still call the seller a robber.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The proposed distinction fails because the stigma persists. The heirs inherit the grievance and will still label the seller a גַּזְלָן. Thus, the motivation to avoid insult remains active.

phrases.aramaic: סוֹף סוֹף קָרוּ לֵיהּ בְּנֵי לוֹקֵחַ ״גַּזְלָנָא״!
phrases.english: Ultimately, ultimately, the purchaser's children called him 'robber'!

steps.triggerLanguage: סוֹף סוֹף קָרוּ לֵיהּ בְּנֵי לוֹקֵחַ ״גַּזְלָנָא״!

steps.title: New distinction: The robber's death
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara proposes a new distinction: if the robber himself died, the 'avoid being called a robber' motivation is moot, but the 'maintain reliability' motivation still applies.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא proposes a new scenario to test the motivations: the robber dies. This setup distinguishes between personal insult and lasting reputation.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If the robber is dead, he cannot be personally insulted. However, the desire to maintain reliability (הֵמָּנוּת) might persist posthumously. This tests if the motivation is tied to the person's existence or their legacy.

phrases.english: Rather: There is between them a similarity of a robber.

phrases.aramaic: מַאן דְּאָמַר נִיחָא לֵיהּ לְאִינִישׁ דְּלָא לִקְרְיוּהּו ״גַּזְלָן״,
phrases.english: The one who says it is preferable for a person that they not call him 'robber',
This one has died.
phrases.notes: Refers to the robber himself having died.

phrases.aramaic: לְמַאן דְּאָמַר נִיחָא לֵיהּ דְּלֵיקוּם בְּהֵמָנוּתֵיהּ,
phrases.english: For the one who says it is preferable that his reliability stand,

phrases.aramaic: הָכִי נָמֵי אַף עַל גַּב דְּמִית –
phrases.english: Thus also even though he died –
It is preferable that his reliability stand!

rashi.hebrew: דמית גזלן - לאחר שלקחה והבנים באים להוציא מיד הלוקח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the scenario of the robber's death applies specifically when the heirs come to evict the buyer from the property after the robber has passed away.

rashi.hebrew: הא מית ליה - ולא נתכוון להעמיד ממכרו אלא בימי חייו שלא יחרפוהו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that for the opinion that the robber wants to avoid being called a robber, this motivation ceases upon his death, as he only cared about avoiding shame during his lifetime.

rashi.hebrew: אע"ג דמית ניחא ליה - מעיקרא:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י notes that the concern for maintaining reliability (הימנותיה) applies from the beginning of the transaction, even if the robber later dies.

rashi.hebrew: דליקו בהימנותיה - אפילו לאחר מיתה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the desire to maintain reliability persists even after the robber's death, as his reputation remains relevant.

steps.title: Challenge: Stigma extends to the robber's children
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges this distinction by noting that the robber's children will be called 'children of a robber' if the sale is invalidated.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא rejects the distinction based on the robber's death. It argues that the stigma extends to his children.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The distinction fails because the insult is not limited to the individual. The children will be labeled 'children of a robber'. Thus, the motivation to avoid family stigma remains active.

phrases.english: In the end, in the end, they call his children: 'Children of robbers.'

rashi.hebrew: סוף סוף קרו לבניה בני גזלנא - נמצא שאף לאחר מיתה מחרפין אותו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's concern is that even after the robber's death, his children will be stigmatized as 'children of a robber', meaning the shame persists posthumously.

steps.title: New distinction: The land was a gift
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara proposes a new distinction: if the robber received the land as a gift, the 'avoid being called a robber' motivation is moot, but the 'maintain reliability' motivation still applies.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא proposes a scenario where the robber received the land as a gift. This removes the financial element of robbery.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If the land was a gift, the robber can claim he stole nothing. The 'avoid insult' motivation is irrelevant here. However, the 'maintain reliability' motivation still applies, as he wants to honor his commitments.

keyTerms.meaning: He gave it as a gift
What did I rob from you?

phrases.english: Rather, there is between them that he gave it as a gift.

phrases.aramaic: מַאן דְּאָמַר נִיחָא לֵיהּ דְּלֵיקוּם בְּהֵמָנוּתֵיהּ –
phrases.english: The one who says it is good for him that he rise in his reliability –

phrases.aramaic: מַתָּנָה נָמֵי נִיחָא לֵיהּ דְּלֵיקוּם בְּהֵמָנוּתֵיהּ.
phrases.english: A gift also it is good for him that he rise in his reliability.

phrases.aramaic: מַאן דְּאָמַר נִיחָא לֵיהּ דְּלָא נִקְרְיוּהּו ״גַּזְלָנָא״ –
phrases.english: The one who says it is good for him that they not call him 'robber' –
He said to him: What did I rob from you?

tosafot.hebrew: מאי גזלינא מינך - וא"ת וכי מכרה נמי מה גזלו והלא צריך להחזיר לו מעותיו וי"ל דמ"מ יקראנו גזלנא כל זמן שלא יפרע לו:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות addresses the difficulty that even in a sale the robber must return the money, so why is he called a גזלן only in the gift case, and answers that he is called a גזלן as long as he has not yet repaid the money.

steps.title: Scenarios lacking intent to validate
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara lists scenarios where the robber's acquisition of the land does not validate the original sale: if he sells it again, bequeaths it, or receives it as an inheritance.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא clarifies scenarios where the robber's acquisition does not validate the sale. These cases lack active intent to establish the transaction.
steps.deeperAnalysis: Inheritance is passive and lacks intent. Selling to a third party or bequeathing also lacks the specific intent to validate the original sale. The key is active effort to establish the sale before the buyer.

keyTerms.meaning: To establish it before the buyer

phrases.english: It is obvious: he sold it, he bequeathed it,
and he gave it as a gift –

phrases.aramaic: לָאו לְאוֹקֹמַהּ קַמֵּי לוֹקֵחַ קָא בָּעֵי.
phrases.english: he does not seek to establish it before the buyer.
It fell to him as an inheritance,
an inheritance is acquired of itself,
and he did not exert effort after it.
He collected it in his debt,

phrases.aramaic: חָזֵינַן אִי אִית לֵיהּ אַרְעָא אַחֲרִיתִי וַאֲמַר:
phrases.english: we see if he has other land and he said:

phrases.aramaic: הַאי בָּעֵינָא, לְאוֹקֹמַהּ קַמֵּיהּ לוֹקֵחַ קָא בָּעֵי.
phrases.english: I want this one, he seeks to establish it before the buyer.

phrases.aramaic: וְאִי לָא, זוּזֵי הוּא דְּבָעֵי אִפְּרוֹעֵי.
phrases.english: And if not, he wants to collect money.

rashi.hebrew: זבנה אורתה - אם קודם שלקח מן הבעלים חזר ומכרה לאיש אחר לבד מן הראשון או הורישה לאחד מבניו או יהבה במתנה ואחר כך לקחה האי ודאי איגלי דעתיה דלאו לאוקמי קמיה לוקח ראשון בעי ולא ניחא לי' דליקום בהימנותיה שהרי מכרה לשני:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that if the robber sells the land to a second party, bequeaths it, or gives it as a gift before acquiring it from the original owner, this demonstrates he does not intend to validate the original sale, as he would not want the first buyer to retain the land.

rashi.hebrew: נפלה ליה - לגזלן בירושה שמתחילה גזלה לאחד ממורישיו ומת הנגזל:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the scenario of inheritance refers to the robber inheriting the stolen land from one of his ancestors who had stolen it from the original owner, who subsequently died.

rashi.hebrew: ירושה ממילא הויא - והא ליכא למימר ניחא ליה דליקו בהימנותיה דהא לא טרח אבתרה דנימא גלי דעתיה דניחא ליה דליקו הלכך הוי כיורש דעלמא וחוזר ותובעה ונותן מעות ללוקח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that inheritance is passive (ירושה ממילא היא), so the robber cannot be said to have actively desired the land to validate the sale, and thus he is treated like a regular heir who must return the land and pay the buyer.

rashi.hebrew: גבייה בחובו - לאחר שגזלה ומכר לו ובא לו אצל נגזל ואמר לו הגבה לי בחובי קרקע שגזלתיך:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that collecting the land as payment for a debt occurs after the robbery and sale, when the robber demands the stolen land from the victim to satisfy a debt owed to him.

rashi.hebrew: אי אית ליה ארעא אחריתי - לנגזל שיכול לגבות חובו ממנה ואמר ליה בזו שגזלתי אני חפץ הא טרח אבתרה ואמרינן לאוקמה קמיה לוקח בעי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that if the victim has other land to pay the debt but the robber specifically demands the stolen land, this active effort (טרח אבתרה) indicates he intends to validate the original sale.

steps.title: Dispute: Is a gift like inheritance or sale?
steps.stepSummary: Rav Acha and Ravina disagree on whether a gift is like an inheritance (passive) or like a sale (active effort to validate).
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא presents a dispute between רַב אַחָא and רַבִּינָא regarding the status of a gift. They disagree on whether receiving a gift requires active effort.
steps.deeperAnalysis: One holds a gift is like inheritance, acquired passively. The other holds it is like a sale, requiring effort to ingratiate oneself. This determines if the gift validates the sale.

keyTerms.meaning: A gift is like a sale

phrases.aramaic: יַהֲבַהּ נִהֲלֵיהּ בְּמַתָּנָה, פְּלִיגִי בַּהּ רַב אַחָא וְרָבִינָא.
phrases.english: He gave it to him as a gift, Rav Acha and Ravina disagree in it.

phrases.aramaic: חַד אָמַר: מַתָּנָה כִּירוּשָּׁה, דְּהָא מִמֵּילָא.
phrases.english: One says: A gift is like an inheritance, for it is of itself.
And one says: A gift is like a sale,

phrases.aramaic: דְּאִי לָאו דְּטָרַח וְאַרְצִי קַמֵּיהּ לָא
phrases.english: for if not that he labored and ingratiated himself before him, he
would not have given him a gift,
therefore he labored and ingratiated himself before him,
in order that he should stand in his reliability.

steps.triggerLanguage: יַהֲבַהּ נִהֲלֵיהּ בְּמַתָּנָה, פְּלִיגִי בַּהּ רַב אַחָא וְרָבִינָא.

rashi.hebrew: יהביה ניהליה במתנה - נגזל לגזלן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the phrase יהביה ניהליה במתנה refers to the victim giving the stolen item to the robber as a gift.

tosafot.hebrew: ארצי קמיה כי היכי דליקום בהימנותיה - משמע דהכי הלכתא מדלא נקט לישנא דלא נקרייה גזלן ועוד דהלכה כרב אשי דהוא בתראה וגדול בחכמה ובמנין:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות derive the הלכה from the Gemara's phrasing that the robber did not ingratiate himself, and also note that the הלכה follows רב אשי as the later and greater authority.

steps.title: Duration of the reliability motivation
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks how long the 'maintain reliability' motivation persists, and offers three opinions: until trial, until court authorization, or until the announcement period.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא questions how long the motivation to maintain reliability lasts. Three opinions define different endpoints in the legal process.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַב הוּנָא says until trial. חִיָּיא בַּר רַב says until court authorization of seizure. רַב פַּפָּא says until announcement days begin. These mark stages where reputation is still at stake.

keyTerms.meaning: Until the time of standing trial

phrases.aramaic: וְעַד אֵימַת נִיחָא לֵיהּ דְּלֵיקוּם בְּהֵמָנוּתֵיהּ?
phrases.english: And until when is it good for him that they go in his reliability?

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר רַב הוּנָא: עַד שְׁעַת הַעֲמָדָה בַּדִּין.
phrases.english: Rav Huna said: Until the time of standing in judgment.

phrases.aramaic: חִיָּיא בַּר רַב אָמַר: עַד דְּמָטְ[יָ]א אַדְרַכְתָּא לִידֵיהּ.
phrases.english: Ḥiyya bar Rav said: Until the writ of seizure comes into his hand.

phrases.aramaic: רַב פָּפָּא אָמַר: עַד דְּמַתְחֲלִן יוֹמֵי אַכְרַזְתָּא.
phrases.english: Rav Pappa said: Until the days of announcement are completed.

steps.triggerLanguage: וְעַד אֵימַת נִיחָא לֵיהּ דְּלֵיקוּם בְּהֵמָנוּתֵיהּ?

rashi.hebrew: ועד אימת - יקחנה מבעלים הראשונים דאמרינן דמשום דניחא ליה דליקו בהימנותיה לקחה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the question 'ועד אימת' refers to how long the robber's motivation to maintain his reputation (ניחא ליה דליקו בהימנותיה) remains effective in causing him to return the item to the original owners.

rashi.hebrew: עד שעת העמדה בדין - עד שיטרפוה מן הלוקח ויעמידהו בדין אבל משעמד ברשעו עד שעת העמדה בדין גלי אדעתיה דלא מהימן הוא ואם לקחה אחרי כן לאו לאוקמיה קמיה לוקח בעי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that according to רב הונא, the motivation lasts until the time of trial (שעת העמדה בדין), because once the robber is exposed as a wrongdoer (עמד ברשעו), his reputation is already damaged, so any subsequent return of the item is not for the sake of maintaining reliability.

rashi.hebrew: עד דמטא אדרכתא לידיה - עד שיחייבוהו ב"ד לפרוע ללוקח מעותיו ועמד במרדו עד שכתבו פסק דין ונתנו ללוקח על נכסיו של גזלן שבכ"מ שימצאם יחזיק בהן והיינו תשעים יום לאחר שעמד בדין כדאמרינן בהגוזל [בתרא] (ב"ק דף קיב:):
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that according to חיyya בר רב, the motivation lasts until the court issues a writ of seizure (אדרכתא) against the robber's property, which occurs after the trial and the robber's defiance, lasting up to ninety days as derived from בבא קמא.

rashi.hebrew: עד דמתחלן יומא דאכרזתא - לאחר שמצא זה קרקע משל גזלן ובא לב"ד והן מכריזין שכל מי שרוצה לקנות קרקע יבא ויקחנה כדאמרינן בערכין (דף כא:) שום היתומין שלשים יום:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that according to רב פפא, the motivation lasts until the days of public announcement (יומי אכרזתא) begin, when the court announces that anyone wishing to purchase the land may do so, similar to the thirty-day announcement period for orphans' property in ערכין.

steps.title: Challenge: The document is merely a shard
steps.stepSummary: Rami bar Chama challenges Rav's ruling by noting that the document is invalid because the robber did not own the land at the time of sale.
steps.whatsHappening: רַמִּי בַּר חָמָא challenges the validity of the document itself. He argues the document is invalid from the outset.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The document is a חַסְפָּא because the robber did not own the land when he sold it. Therefore, subsequent acquisition cannot retroactively validate an inherently invalid document.

phrases.english: Rami bar Ḥama objects to it:

phrases.aramaic: מִכְּדִי הַאי לוֹקֵחַ בְּמַאי קָנֵי לְהַאי אַרְעָא –
phrases.english: Since this purchaser — by what does he acquire this land?

phrases.aramaic: בְּהַאי שְׁטָרָא, הַאי שְׁטָרָא חַסְפָּא בְּעָלְמָא הוּא!
phrases.english: By this deed? This deed is merely a shard!
phrases.notes: A 'shard' refers to a document with no legal validity, as the seller did not own the property.

rashi.hebrew: מתקיף לה רמי בר חמא - אהא מה מכר ראשון לשני כו':
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that רמי בר חמא's challenge is directed specifically at Rav's ruling that the first seller sold the land to the second buyer.

tosafot.hebrew: האי שטרא חספא בעלמא הוא - וא"ת ולימא דקני לה במה שהחזיק בה אחר שקנאה גזלן וי"ל דמשמע ליה משעה שקנאה הגזלן היא של לוקח אע"פ שלא עשה בו שום מעשה וא"ת מעותיו שהיו תחלה מלוה נעשו עתה מכר וי"ל באתרא דלא קנו בכספא א"נ במלוה אינו יכול לקנות כדאמרינן פ"ק דקדושין (דף ו:) המקדש במלוה אינה מקודשת ובמכר לא קנה והא דאמר בהאיש מקדש (שם דף מז.) ושוין שבמכר קנה היינו במלוה שיש לו על אחרים דאז באשה בענין זה מקודשת לכ"ע אי לאו דאשה לא סמכא דעתה ויראה שיחזור וימחול אבל מלוה שאשה חייבת לו אם מוחל לה ומקדשה בה אינה מקודשת דלא נתן לה כלום דמקמי הכי היה ברשותה וכן במכר לא קנה אע"ג דכשמקדשה בהנאת מחילת מלוה היא מקודשת דלא גרע מארווח לה זמנא ואדבר עליך לשלטון דמקודשת וכן במכר אם מכר בהנאת מחילה קונה כמו בהחליף דמי שור בפרה מ"מ לא משמע ליה שהיה מקנה לו בהנאת מחילת מלוה משום דמשמע מיד שלקחה הגזלן קנה לוקח ראשון:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות addresses why the buyer didn't acquire the land through חזקה after the robber acquired it, explaining that רמי בר חמא understands the buyer's acquisition to be immediate upon the robber's acquisition without any act of חזקה.

steps.title: Resolution based on the buyer's trust
steps.stepSummary: Rava resolves the challenge by suggesting that the buyer's trust in the robber validates the document.
steps.whatsHappening: רַבָּא resolves the challenge by introducing the concept of the buyer's trust. This is a setup for the next challenge.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַבָּא suggests that the buyer's trust in the robber validates the document. The robber's satisfaction from this trust motivates him to acquire the land and transfer it, thereby validating the sale.

keyTerms.meaning: Let it be in a case where he trusts him

keyTerms.term: בְּהָהִיא הֲנָאָה דְּלָא קָאָמַר לֵיהּ מִידֵי
keyTerms.meaning: By that satisfaction that he did not say anything to him

phrases.english: Rava said to him: Let it be in his trust,

phrases.aramaic: בְּהָהִיא הֲנָאָה דְּלָא קָאָמַר לֵיהּ מִידֵּי
phrases.english: in that benefit that he did not say anything to him

phrases.aramaic: וְקָא סָמֵיךְ עֲלֵיהּ טָרַח וּמַיְיתֵי לֵיהּ,
phrases.english: and he relies on him, he takes trouble and brings to him,
he concludes and acquires to him.

rashi.hebrew: תהא - הא דרב:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that רב's ruling is the basis for the resolution.

rashi.hebrew: במאמינו - שאמר לו אני סומך עליך שתתננה בידי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that במאמינו refers to the buyer's declaration of trust that the robber will deliver the land.

rashi.hebrew: גמר ומקני - כשלקחה מבעלים הראשונים:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that גמר ומקני refers to the robber's acquisition of the land from the original owners.

tosafot.hebrew: בההיא הנאה דקא סמיך כו' - והויא כאילו א"ל שדה זו תהא קנויה לך לכשאקחנה:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that the buyer's trust creates a benefit for the robber, functioning as if the robber had already promised the land would be acquired for the buyer.

steps.title: Challenge from a baraita on selling future acquisitions
steps.stepSummary: Rav Sheshet challenges Rav's ruling from a baraita that states one cannot sell future acquisitions unless specified as 'today'.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב שֵׁשֶׁת challenges רַב's ruling from a baraita that limits the validity of selling future acquisitions. This is a direct challenge to the core principle of the sugya.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The baraita states that one cannot sell what they will inherit or catch in the future unless they specify 'today'. This contradicts רַב's ruling that the robber's subsequent acquisition validates the sale without such specification.

keyTerms.meaning: What I will inherit from my father is sold to you

phrases.english: Rav Sheshet raises an objection:
That which I will inherit from my father is sold to you,
That which my net will catch is sold to you –
He has said nothing.

phrases.aramaic: מַה שֶּׁאִירַשׁ מִן אַבָּא הַיּוֹם מָכוּר לָךְ,
phrases.english: That which I will inherit from my father today is sold to you,

phrases.aramaic: מַה שֶּׁתַּעֲלֶה מְצוּדָתִי הַיּוֹם מָכוּר לָךְ –
phrases.english: That which my net will catch today is sold to you –
His words stand!

rashi.hebrew: מצודתי - של חיות ועופות ושל דגים:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the term מְצוּדָתִי in the baraita refers to the future acquisition of animals, birds, or fish.

rashi.hebrew: דבריו קיימין - לקמן פריך מאי שנא רישא כו':
rashi.takeaway: רש״י notes that the Gemara will subsequently challenge the distinction between the first and second clauses of this baraita.

tosafot.hebrew: מה שאירש מאבא מכור לך לא אמר כלום - וא"ת לימא דרב דאמר כר"מ וברייתא כרבנן וי"ל דלדידן קפריך דאית לן כרב כדאיתא פרק איזהו נשך (לקמן בבא מציעא דף עב:) דמייתי גמרא טעמא דלא ליקריה גזלנא וניחא ליה דליקו בהימנותיה וסבירא לן כרבנן דאין אדם מקנה דבר שלא בא לעולם דשמואל פסיק כרבי יוחנן הסנדלר פרק אע"פ (כתובות דף נט(א,ב).) ורב נחמן סבר בפירות דקל אף משבאו לעולם יכול לחזור בו ורב האי פסק דאין הלכה כרב בהא משום דס"ל דאין אדם מקנה דבר שלא בא לעולם וכו' ולא נהירא מכח דאיזהו נשך (לקמן בבא מציעא דף עב:):
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות addresses the potential resolution that רב follows רבי מאיר while the baraita follows the רבנן, but rejects it because we follow רב's leniency in איזהו נשך and the רבנן's strictness regarding דבר שלא בא לעולם, as supported by שמואל and רב נחמן.

steps.title: רַמִּי בַּר חָמָא's endorsement of the challenge
steps.stepSummary: Rami bar Chama endorses the challenge, calling Rav Sheshet a 'great man' and the refutation compelling.
steps.whatsHappening: רַמִּי בַּר חָמָא endorses the challenge, praising רַב שֵׁשֶׁת and the refutation. This is a rhetorical endorsement, not a new argument.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַמִּי בַּר חָמָא uses the phrase 'הָא גַּבְרָא וְהָא תְּיוּבְתָּא' to express his agreement with the challenge. This sets up רַבָּא's response.

keyTerms.meaning: This is the great man and this is his refutation

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר רָמֵי בַּר חָמָא: הָא גַּבְרָא וְהָא תְּיוּבְתָּא!
phrases.english: Rami bar Ḥama said: This is the man and this is his refutation!

rashi.hebrew: הא גברא והא תיובתא - הרי אדם גדול וכמוהו תשובתו דקתני לא אמר כלום ולכשיירש לא קנה לוקח כיון דההיא שעתא לאו דידיה הוו נכסי אביו ולא אמרינן מה מכר לו ראשון לשני כל זכות שתבא לידו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רמי בר חמא's phrase 'הא גברא והא תיובתא' praises both the sage (רב ששת) and the refutation, and clarifies that the refutation proves the buyer does not acquire the inheritance because the property was not the father's at the time of sale, thus invalidating the principle of 'מה שמכר לו ראשון לשני כל זכות שתבא לידו'.

steps.title: רַבָּא's distinction based on the buyer's reliance
steps.stepSummary: Rava distinguishes between the robber's case and the baraita's case based on the buyer's reliance on the seller.
steps.whatsHappening: רַבָּא distinguishes between the two cases based on the buyer's reliance. This is a subtle distinction that hinges on the psychological state of the buyer.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַבָּא argues that in the robber's case, the buyer relies on the seller to acquire the land and validate the sale. In the baraita's case, the buyer does not rely on the seller because the acquisition is uncertain.

keyTerms.meaning: Here, he did not rely on him

phrases.english: Rava said: I see a great man,
but I do not see a conclusive refutation.

phrases.aramaic: הָכָא סָמְכָא דַּעְתֵּיהּ, וְהָכָא לָא סָמְכָא דַּעְתֵּיהּ.
phrases.english: Here he relies on him, and there he does not rely on him.
Here he relies on him –

phrases.aramaic: דְּאָזֵיל טָרַח וּמַיְיתֵי לֵיהּ כִּי הֵיכִי,
phrases.english: that he goes, takes the trouble, and brings to him in such a way,
that they do not call him 'a robber'.
Here he does not rely on him.

rashi.hebrew: לא סמכא דעתיה - דלוקח דקאמר מי יימר שירש מאביו כלום שמא ימכור אביו נכסיו בחייו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the buyer does not rely on the seller in the baraita's case because the seller might not inherit anything from his father, as the father could sell the property while still alive.

steps.title: Dispute on the strength of the challenge
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara presents a dispute between Rabbi Abba bar Zavda and Rava on whether the challenge is compelling.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא presents a dispute on whether the challenge is compelling. This is a meta-dispute on the validity of the challenge itself.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַבִּי אַבָּא בַר זַבְדָּא says the challenge is clear and compelling. רַבָּא disagrees, saying it needs further examination.

keyTerms.meaning: This does not need to be brought inside
This needs to be brought inside and inside the innermost area

phrases.aramaic: שַׁלְחוּהָ לְקַמֵּיהּ דְּרַבִּי אַבָּא בַּר זַבְדָּא.
phrases.english: They sent it before Rabbi Abba bar Zavda.

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַר לְהוּ: זוֹ אֵינָהּ צְרִיכָה לִפְנִים.
phrases.english: He said to them: This does not need to be brought inside.

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר רָבָא: זוֹ צְרִיכָה לִפְנִים וְלִפְנַי לִפְנִים.
phrases.english: Rava said: This needs to be brought inside and inside the inside.

phrases.aramaic: הָכָא סָמְכָא דַּעְתֵּיהּ, וְהָכָא לָא סָמְכָא דַּעְתֵּיהּ.
phrases.english: Here he relies on his mind, and here he does not rely on his mind.

steps.triggerLanguage: שַׁלְחוּהָ לְקַמֵּיהּ דְּרַבִּי אַבָּא בַר זַבְדָּא

rashi.hebrew: שלחוה - להא תיובתא דרב ששת דאותיב אדרב:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the challenge (תיובתא) was sent to רבי אבא בר זבדא because it refutes רב ששת, who had challenged רב.

rashi.hebrew: אינה צריכה לפנים - אינה צריכה כדי לתרצה להכניסה לפני בני הישיבה שאין בהן יודע לפרקה ותשובת הגאונים פורשין לגנאי דליכא קושיא מעליא אל תעלוה לגיו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבי אבא בר זבדא's statement 'זו אינה צריכה לפנים' means the challenge is so weak it does not even warrant being brought before the ישיבה for resolution, as the גאונים interpret it as a dismissal of a poor objection.

steps.title: Practical application in פומבדיתא
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara relates an incident in Pumbedita where the court ruled according to Rav, and Rav Yosef and Abaye disagree on the strength of the challenge.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא relates a practical application of the dispute in פומבדיתא. This is a historical account of a court ruling.
steps.deeperAnalysis: In פומבדיתא, the court ruled according to רַב. רַב יוֹסֵף agreed with רַבִּי אַבָּא בַר זַבְדָּא that the challenge is compelling. אַבָּיֵי agreed with רַבָּא that the challenge needs further examination.

keyTerms.meaning: There was an incident in פומבדיתא

phrases.english: There was an incident in Pumbedita and they ruled.

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַר לְהוּ רַב יוֹסֵף: זוֹ אֵינָהּ צְרִיכָה לְפָנִים.
phrases.english: Rav Yosef said to them: This does not need to be brought inside.
And Abaye said to him:
It needs to be brought inside and inside the inside,
Here he relied on his mind,
Here he did not rely on his mind.

rashi.hebrew: הוה עובדא בפומבדיתא - שדנו דין כרב ואותבינן מהא מתניתין ואמר רב יוסף כו':
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the court in פומבדיתא ruled according to רב, and the objection raised was based on the מיתא, which רב יוסף addressed.

steps.title: Reason for the distinction in the baraita
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks for the reason behind the distinction in the baraita between selling future acquisitions generally and selling them 'today'.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא asks for the reason behind the distinction in the baraita. This is a final explanation of the baraita.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן explains that the distinction is based on the honor of the father (for inheritance) and the seller's livelihood (for fishing). In these cases, the Sages instituted an ordinance to validate the sale.

keyTerms.meaning: Because of the honor of his father

phrases.aramaic: וּמַאי שְׁנָא רֵישָׁא וּמַאי שְׁנָא סֵיפָא?
phrases.english: And what is different in the first clause, and what is different in the latter…
Rabbi Yoḥanan said:
The latter clause, "that which I will inherit from my father today" –
Because of the honor of his father.

phrases.aramaic: ״מַה שֶּׁתַּעֲלֶה מְצוּדָתִי הַיּוֹם״ – מִשּׁוּם כְּדֵי חַיָּיו.
phrases.english: "That which my net will catch today" – because of his livelihood.

steps.triggerLanguage: וּמַאי שְׁנָא רֵישָׁא וּמַאי שְׁנָא סֵיפָא?

rashi.hebrew: משום כבוד אביו - כשהיה אביו גוסס וצריך מעות לקבורתו ולתכריכין מוזהר לכבוד אביו שלא לשהותו בבזיון:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the concern for כבוד אביו arises when the father is on his deathbed and requires funds for burial and shrouds, obligating the son to act promptly to avoid dishonoring him.
